seo.title: E-Commerce Content Generation AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for content generation AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for e-commerce content generation introduces compounding risks: a single hallucinated product claim or inaccurate price can trigger FTC enforcement, fuel refund disputes, and erode customer trust at scale. Because LLMs generate content dynamically, traditional one-time editorial reviews are insufficient — every output pipeline must embed real-time compliance checks, brand guardrails, and factual verification. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls to ensure your AI content system meets FTC advertising standards, CCPA/GDPR data obligations, and ADA accessibility requirements before a single word reaches a customer.

sections.heading: Product Claims & Factual Accuracy Controls
sections.description: AI-generated product descriptions and marketing copy must be grounded in verified, substantiated data. Unverified superlatives, false comparisons, or fabricated specifications expose your brand to FTC enforcement and customer disputes.

items.label: Implement a product data grounding layer
items.description: Connect the LLM to a live, authoritative product catalog API so all specifications, dimensions, materials, and features are retrieved from a verified source rather than generated from model weights. Reject any output that references a product attribute not present in the catalog record.

items.label: Block unsubstantiated superlative claims
items.description: Configure a post-generation filter using a regex and classifier combination to flag phrases like 'best in class,' '#1 rated,' or 'clinically proven' unless a corresponding substantiation record (e.g., third-party test result, verified review aggregate) is attached in the prompt context.

items.label: Enforce real-time price accuracy validation
items.description: Before publishing any AI-generated content containing a price, query the pricing microservice to confirm the displayed price matches the current transactional price. Automatically suppress or regenerate content if a discrepancy exceeds $0.01 to prevent deceptive pricing violations.

items.label: Validate return and refund policy representations
items.description: Maintain a structured policy data store (return windows, restocking fees, exclusions) and inject the relevant policy snippet into every prompt that may generate return-related copy. Run a semantic similarity check between generated text and the canonical policy to flag misrepresentations above a 0.85 cosine distance threshold.

items.label: Log all generated claims with source citations
items.description: For every published piece of AI-generated content, store a structured audit record linking each factual claim to its source data point (catalog field, policy record, or approved marketing brief). Retain logs for a minimum of 24 months to support FTC substantiation requests.

items.label: Schedule automated re-validation on inventory changes
items.description: Trigger a re-validation job whenever a product's catalog record is updated (price change, spec revision, discontinuation). Automatically unpublish or flag AI-generated content referencing the changed product until a human editor approves the refreshed version.

items.label: Implement a comparative advertising review gate
items.description: Route any AI-generated content that names a competitor or makes a comparative claim (e.g., 'faster than Brand X') to a mandatory human legal review queue before publication, as comparative claims require documented substantiation under FTC guidelines.

sections.heading: Brand Voice, Tone & Originality Enforcement
sections.description: Consistent brand voice builds customer trust and differentiates your catalog. AI agents must adhere to editorial guidelines, avoid plagiarism, and maintain tone standards across thousands of simultaneous content outputs.

items.label: Encode brand voice as a versioned system prompt artifact
items.description: Store your brand voice guidelines (tone adjectives, prohibited phrases, sentence length targets, persona attributes) as a versioned JSON artifact injected into every LLM system prompt. Increment the version on any guideline change and log which version produced each piece of content for rollback traceability.

items.label: Run tone adherence scoring on every output
items.description: After generation, pass each content piece through a fine-tuned tone classifier trained on approved brand examples. Set a minimum acceptable tone score (e.g., 0.80 confidence) and automatically route below-threshold outputs to a human editor queue rather than publishing directly.

items.label: Integrate originality checking before publication
items.description: Submit all AI-generated long-form content (product descriptions over 150 words, blog posts, category pages) to a plagiarism detection API (e.g., Copyscape or equivalent) and block publication of any content with more than 15% similarity to existing indexed web content.

items.label: Maintain a prohibited terms and phrases blocklist
items.description: Compile a regularly updated blocklist of legally sensitive terms (e.g., 'guaranteed cure,' 'FDA approved' without verification, competitor brand names used improperly) and apply a pre-publication filter that hard-blocks content containing any listed term.

items.label: Enforce SEO compliance without keyword stuffing
items.description: Define maximum keyword density thresholds (e.g., primary keyword ≤ 2.5% of total word count) and validate generated content against these thresholds automatically. Flag content that exceeds limits, as keyword stuffing can trigger search engine penalties and degrade user experience.

items.label: Implement A/B content variant tracking
items.description: When the AI generates multiple content variants for testing, tag each variant with a unique content ID and associate it with the specific prompt version, model version, and brand voice artifact version. This enables attribution of performance and compliance issues to specific generation parameters.

items.label: Conduct quarterly brand voice drift audits
items.description: Every 90 days, sample 200 randomly selected AI-generated content pieces and score them against the current brand voice rubric using both automated classifiers and a human editorial panel. Document drift metrics and retrain or update system prompts if average tone scores decline by more than 5 points.

sections.heading: Data Privacy, Consent & CCPA/GDPR Compliance
sections.description: E-commerce AI content systems often ingest customer behavioral data, purchase history, and browsing signals to personalize outputs. This data use must comply with CCPA and GDPR consent, minimization, and deletion requirements.

items.label: Audit all customer data inputs to the LLM pipeline
items.description: Document every data field (e.g., purchase history, browsing behavior, location, demographic segments) fed into prompts for personalized content generation. Map each field to its legal basis under GDPR Article 6 or CCPA category, and remove any field lacking a documented lawful basis before the next deployment cycle.

items.label: Implement PII scrubbing before LLM prompt injection
items.description: Deploy an automated PII detection layer (using a tool such as Microsoft Presidio or AWS Comprehend) that scans all prompt inputs and replaces direct identifiers (name, email, address, payment card data) with anonymized tokens before the data reaches the LLM API endpoint.

items.label: Honor opt-out signals in personalization pipelines
items.description: Integrate a real-time consent status check into the content generation pipeline. If a customer has exercised CCPA opt-out of sale/sharing or GDPR objection rights, the system must fall back to non-personalized, catalog-only content generation for that user within 15 business days of the request.

items.label: Exclude deleted user data from training and fine-tuning
items.description: Establish a data deletion propagation process that removes records of users who have submitted CCPA deletion requests or GDPR erasure requests from any fine-tuning datasets, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) indexes, or prompt caches within 30 days of the verified request.

items.label: Disclose AI-generated content to consumers where required
items.description: Review applicable state laws and platform terms to determine disclosure obligations for AI-generated marketing content. Where required, add a visible disclosure label (e.g., 'Content created with AI assistance') to product pages and email campaigns generated by the LLM pipeline.

items.label: Conduct a DPIA for high-volume personalized content generation
items.description: Complete a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) under GDPR Article 35 if the AI system processes behavioral data at scale to generate individualized content. Document the assessment, mitigation measures, and DPO sign-off before go-live.

items.label: Restrict LLM vendor data retention and training use
items.description: Review your LLM API vendor's data processing agreement to confirm that prompt data containing customer signals is not retained beyond the session or used to train shared models. Obtain a signed Data Processing Addendum (DPA) that explicitly prohibits training use of your prompt data.

sections.heading: ADA Accessibility, Human Oversight & Incident Response
sections.description: AI-generated content must meet ADA and WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards, and the deployment must include human oversight mechanisms and a documented incident response plan to address content failures before they escalate.

items.label: Auto-generate and validate alt text for all AI-produced images
items.description: When the AI pipeline generates or selects product images, automatically produce descriptive alt text using a vision-language model and validate that the alt text is between 50–150 characters, describes the image accurately, and does not begin with 'image of' or 'photo of' per WCAG 2.1 guideline 1.1.1.

items.label: Enforce reading level and plain language standards
items.description: Run all AI-generated customer-facing copy through a Flesch-Kincaid readability scorer and target a Grade 8 or below reading level for product descriptions and policy summaries. Flag content scoring above Grade 10 for simplification to ensure accessibility for users with cognitive disabilities.

items.label: Validate heading structure and semantic HTML in generated content
items.description: When AI-generated content is injected into page templates, run an automated HTML validation check to confirm proper heading hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3), no skipped heading levels, and correct use of semantic elements (lists, tables with headers) per WCAG 2.1 guideline 1.3.1.

items.label: Establish a human-in-the-loop review gate for high-stakes content
items.description: Define a content risk tier system: Tier 1 (homepage hero copy, promotional pricing, health/safety claims) requires mandatory human editorial approval before publication; Tier 2 (standard product descriptions) uses automated checks with a 5% random human spot-check sample; Tier 3 (meta descriptions, alt text) is fully automated with weekly audits.

items.label: Create a content incident response runbook
items.description: Document a step-by-step runbook for content incidents (e.g., incorrect price published at scale, false product claim indexed by search engines). The runbook must specify: detection trigger, escalation contacts, takedown SLA (target: within 2 hours of detection), customer notification criteria, and FTC self-reporting evaluation checklist.

items.label: Implement a content kill switch for bulk takedown
items.description: Build an operational control that allows a designated compliance officer to immediately suppress all AI-generated content associated with a specific product ID, campaign tag, or model version across all channels (website, email, ads) within 30 minutes of activation, without requiring a full deployment cycle.

items.label: Track and report AI content performance and error rates monthly
items.description: Produce a monthly dashboard reporting: volume of AI-generated content published, percentage flagged by automated checks, percentage escalated to human review, number of post-publication corrections, and any customer complaints attributable to AI content errors. Share with legal, compliance, and product leadership.

items.label: Test accessibility of AI-generated content with assistive technology quarterly
items.description: Every quarter, conduct manual testing of a representative sample of AI-generated product pages using NVDA/JAWS screen readers and keyboard-only navigation. Document failures against WCAG 2.1 AA criteria and create remediation tickets with a 30-day resolution SLA.

notes.regulation: FTC Advertising Guidelines & Endorsement Guides (16 CFR Part 255)
notes.note: The FTC's 2023 updated Endorsement Guides explicitly extend to AI-generated reviews and testimonials. Any AI-generated content that simulates customer reviews, uses synthetic testimonials, or makes performance claims must include clear and conspicuous disclosures. The FTC's substantiation standard requires that objective product claims be supported by competent and reliable evidence before publication — not after a complaint is received. Penalties for deceptive AI-generated advertising can reach $50,120 per violation per day.

notes.regulation: CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) & CPRA Amendments
notes.note: Under CCPA as amended by CPRA, using customer purchase history or behavioral data to personalize AI-generated content may constitute 'sharing' of personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising, triggering opt-out rights even without monetary exchange. E-commerce operators must implement a 'Do Not Share My Personal Information' signal and ensure the AI content pipeline respects Global Privacy Control (GPC) browser signals automatically, not just manual opt-out form submissions.

notes.regulation: PCI DSS v4.0 — Scope Boundary for AI Systems
notes.note: If the AI content generation system is hosted in the same environment as cardholder data or payment processing infrastructure, it falls within PCI DSS scope and requires compliance with requirements 6 (secure systems development), 8 (identity management), and 12 (information security policies). Ensure the LLM API calls are routed through a network segment that is explicitly out of scope for cardholder data, and document the scope boundary in your annual PCI DSS assessment to avoid unnecessary compliance burden.

notes.regulation: ADA Title III & WCAG 2.1 AA — Digital Accessibility for E-Commerce
notes.note: Federal courts have consistently held that e-commerce websites are 'places of public accommodation' under ADA Title III, and AI-generated content that is inaccessible to users with disabilities creates direct legal exposure. The Department of Justice's March 2022 guidance confirms WCAG 2.1 AA as the applicable standard. AI-generated content pipelines must treat accessibility as a pre-publication requirement, not a post-launch remediation task, as demand letters and class action filings targeting inaccessible product pages have increased by over 300% since 2018.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI content pipeline against FTC, CCPA, and ADA requirements — catching compliance gaps before they become enforcement actions or customer disputes.
